findings.title: Potential shell or process execution
findings.detail: This code invokes a shell or subprocess API that can execute arbitrary commands.
findings.remediation: Review whether shell execution is required and restrict command construction or block the package.

findings.title: Potential outbound network access
findings.detail: This code uses networking libraries that may exfiltrate data or call undeclared endpoints.
findings.remediation: Require an outbound allowlist and review target domains before allowing execution.

findings.title: Potential filesystem mutation
findings.detail: This code writes to or deletes files and directories.
findings.remediation: Run in a restricted mount profile and review whether write access is actually necessary.

findings.title: Potential secret or environment access
findings.detail: This code reads environment variables that may include credentials or host secrets.
findings.remediation: Inject only explicit environment variables and short-lived credentials at runtime.

rationale: Shell execution was detected, so direct host execution should not be allowed.
Outbound networking was detected, so allowlists or full denial should be enforced.

recommendedActions: Run this MCP server only inside an isolated runtime and require manual review of command execution paths.
Apply an outbound domain allowlist and review every declared remote dependency.
Limit writable mounts to a dedicated temp directory and keep the project workspace read-only by default.
Inject only explicit, short-lived credentials instead of passing through the host environment.
Require manual approval before installation or upgrade.
Deny remote MCP access unless ownership and target domains are explicitly verified.
